growth_records.cite: Xie et al. The insertion of the inverted repeat of an insertion sequence (IS) element from Lacticaseibacillus rhamnosus changes the host range and stability of pGK12, a shuttle vector for lactic acid bacteria. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.1128/aem.01908-24. [PMC12016507]
growth_records.snippet: Under non-selective conditions, L. paracasei ATCC 334 with no plasmid had the fastest growth rate (mu max, no plasmid = 0.1067 +/- 0.0035 h-1). ... TABLE 2 Maximum growth rate (h-1) No plasmid ... L. paracasei ATCC 334 0.1067 +/- 0.0035
growth_records.notes: Plasmid-free host strain, MRS broth, 37C, anaerobic (Table 2). mu_max = 0.1067 +/- 0.0035 h-1 (n=3 biological replicates), measured as slope at inflection of logistic fit to OD595 growth curve. Doubling time = ln2/0.1067 = 6.50 h (calculated). No uptake/secretion/MFA reported (plasmid host-range study). MRS composition not stated (commercial medium name only). For reference, plasmid-bearing variants of this strain (same condition): pGK12 mu=0.0690, pTRK829 mu=0.0854, pTRK830 mu=0.0732 h-1.
